summary:
Admin allowlist now checks the stored username and registration strips BIDI control characters so spoofed names like “‮nimda” can’t escalate to admin.

- `app/auth.py:133-154` compare session usernames directly against `ADMIN_USERS`, keeping the rendered display name only for messaging so BIDI overrides can’t bypass the allowlist.
- `app/sanitizer.py:24-74` remove the known Unicode BIDI control set before the existing HTML/script sanitizers run, ensuring the canonical username stays stable for storage and login.

Tests: `./test.sh`

You’re clear to deploy; optionally 1) add regression coverage for other format-control characters if this system accepts multi-language usernames.
